unit: ev-loop-interactive § Scope-shift detection — inner-RPI accept path

contract.goal: Modify skills/ev-loop-interactive/SKILL.md step 5 (Scope-shift detection) so the accept path runs the inner-RPI sub-sequence instead of today's single Revise PLAN.md call. The existing two-signal-concurrence rule is preserved verbatim; what changes is what happens AFTER concurrence fires + accept: emit rpi-inner-triggered → spawn /loom-research via Agent tool (--mode=auto, brief = the trigger rationale) → on success spawn /loom-revise-plan via Agent tool (--flavor=research, --mode=auto) → re-read manifest → emit rpi-inner-completed → continue to phase update. Also adds scope-shift-detected on every detected shift (regardless of accept/decline), rpi-inner-declined on decline, and a clean sub-agent failure flow that exits the unit without writing a parent RECOVERY-STATUS.json (the sub-agent already did).
acceptance_criteria: Step 5 signal-source list (4 sources) and two-signal-concurrence rule preserved verbatim.
scope-shift-detected emitted on EVERY detected shift (when 2+ signals concur) with detail {slug, phase, unit, signal_count, signals: string[]}.

6-step inner-RPI sub-sequence documented in order with explicit Agent-tool spawns + --mode=auto / --flavor=research args + manifest re-read + deleted-phase handling + explicit 'Do not re-execute the current unit'.
rpi-inner-declined emitted on decline with detail {slug, phase, signal_count}; signals appended to notes_for_pr.

Sub-agent failure flow: parent does NOT write own RECOVERY-STATUS.json; surfaces failure path + step; exits unit cleanly; does NOT emit rpi-inner-completed (so events.jsonl shows triggered-without-completed as the partial-failure signal).
Auto-mode default for the accept/decline question flips to ACCEPT (auto-mode treats two-signal concurrence as enough evidence).
Skill preamble updated with a 'Spawns' line naming /loom-research + /loom-revise-plan on the inner-RPI accept path.
npm test green at 485 (skill body is markdown — no test surface change).
rules_applied: PLAN.md Phase 5 lines 379-416 dictates the inner-RPI flow verbatim — including the 'Do not re-execute the unit' constraint.
Substrate composition: Agent tool for fresh-context sub-agent spawns; Skill tool for inherited-context composition.

Sub-agent failure recovery is the SUB-agent's responsibility (it writes RECOVERY-STATUS.json); the parent surfaces and exits — different shape from /loom-plan where the parent owns recovery for its spawn of /loom-research.
CLAUDE.md: keep the load-bearing existing behavior unchanged (two-signal rule); only modify the accept path.
disqualifiers: Writing the parent's own RECOVERY-STATUS.json on sub-agent failure.
Re-executing the current unit after the inner-RPI sequence.
inputs: skills/ev-loop-interactive/SKILL.md (pre-change at lines 269-307: the existing scope-shift step)
cli/lib/types.ts (D1 of this phase — the 4 event types this step emits)

actions: Updated the preamble: added a new 'Spawns' line listing /loom-research + /loom-revise-plan as Agent-tool fresh-context spawns on the inner-RPI accept path. Kept the existing 'Composes' line + 'Does not compose: other loops' framing intact (Skill-tool composition vs Agent-tool spawning are still distinct surfaces).

Rewrote step 5 of the unit loop: preserved the 4 signal sources + two-signal-concurrence rule verbatim. Added a scope-shift-detected emission paragraph after the rule (fires on EVERY detected shift, gated by two-signal-concurrence). Rewrote 'On accept' as a 6-step numbered sub-sequence: emit rpi-inner-triggered → spawn /loom-research via Agent tool → spawn /loom-revise-plan via Agent tool → re-read manifest with deleted-phase handling → emit rpi-inner-completed → continue to phase update. Added 'Do not re-execute the current unit' verbatim per PLAN.md.

Authored the sub-agent failure flow: parent does NOT write its own RECOVERY-STATUS.json (the sub-agent already did); surfaces the failure path + step to the operator; exits unit cleanly; does NOT emit rpi-inner-completed. Documented the events.jsonl partial-failure signal shape (rpi-inner-triggered without matching rpi-inner-completed).

Added auto-mode accept-default flip in the offer-flow paragraph: human-paired default is decline (no interrupt unless user accepts); auto-mode flips to accept (two-signal concurrence is sufficient evidence).
Added rpi-inner-declined emission on decline + an explicit append of signals to notes_for_pr so the substrate captures detection even when not acted on.
Noted that scope-shift-detected does NOT fire on single-signal path (gated by two-signal-concurrence alongside the offer).
Ran `npm test`: 485/485 passed (markdown-only change to skill body).
Derived evaluator panel: contract-fit (substrate-only file). Invoked /guild-validate. Verdict: approved on first round. All 5 spot-check items verified.

changes_since_previous: Second unit on this branch. D1 declared the 4 inner-RPI event types. D2 wires the loop body to emit them via the new accept-path behavior.
notes_for_pr: The accept path is now the inner-RPI sub-sequence: previously a single § Revise PLAN.md call, now a 6-step orchestration spawning /loom-research → /loom-revise-plan. The change is bounded to the accept path; everything else in step 5 (signal sources, two-signal rule, single-signal note, zero-signal no-op) is preserved verbatim.

Sub-agent failure flow is DIFFERENT from /loom-plan's failure handling: /loom-plan owns recovery for its spawn of /loom-research (writes parent's RECOVERY-STATUS.json with sub_agent_recovery_path back-reference). Here, the loop body just surfaces and exits; the next /ev-run invocation will detect the sub-agent's RECOVERY-STATUS.json and offer to resume. The asymmetry is intentional — /loom-plan IS the outer orchestrator (recovery is its job); the ev-loop is mid-execution and the next /ev-run can handle the sub-agent's recovery natively.

Auto-mode default flip: human-paired defaults to decline (no interruption unless user explicitly accepts); auto-mode defaults to accept (two-signal concurrence IS the evidence threshold). Consistent with the substrate's broader auto-mode posture — auto-mode accepts panel resolutions unless budgets exhaust.

The scope-shift-detected event fires only on two-signal concurrence (not on single-signal). This is a substrate-trace decision: single-signal notes already land in notes_for_pr; an event-stream record of every single-signal observation would flood events.jsonl with low-signal entries. Documented in the 'On single signal' branch so a future reader doesn't expect the event there.

I'm running under this very loop body during this session. The edit doesn't affect my current execution (the loop body is loaded once at session start per L-004); the new accept-path behavior takes effect for future /ev-run / /ev-loop-interactive invocations. No risk of mid-session breakage from the change.
